title: North African Spiced Shepherd's Pie
description: A harissa-spiced lentil and mushroom filling with a golden olive oil mash — warming, hearty, and even better the next day.

steps.title: Cook the lentils
steps.content: Rinse the lentils and simmer in plenty of water for 20–25 minutes until just tender but still holding their shape. Drain and set aside.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.content: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 30 seconds, then add leek, celery and carrots. Cook for 8–10 minutes until softened.

steps.title: Add garlic and spices
steps.content: Add garlic, ground cumin and rose harissa paste. Stir well and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.

steps.title: Add mushrooms
steps.content: Add mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes until they've released their moisture and started to colour.

steps.title: Build the sauce
steps.content: Add tinned tomatoes, vegetable stock and miso paste. Stir to combine, then add the cooked lentils and white beans. Simmer on a low heat for 15–20 minutes until thick and rich. It should not be sloppy — let it reduce properly. Finish with lemon juice and season well.

steps.title: Make the mash
steps.content: While the filling simmers, boil potatoes in salted water until tender, around 20 minutes. Drain thoroughly, then mash with olive oil and a splash of the cooking water if needed. Season generously. You want it smooth and spreadable but not too loose.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.content: Preheat oven to 200°C. Spoon the filling into a baking dish. Spread the mash over the top evenly, then rough up the surface with a fork to create ridges — these will catch and brown nicely. Drizzle a little extra olive oil over the top.

steps.title: Bake
steps.content: Bake for 25–30 minutes until the top is golden and the filling is bubbling at the edges. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

notes: The filling genuinely improves overnight as the harissa and spices develop. If reheating the next day, add a splash of water or stock before putting it back in the oven covered with foil, then uncover for the last 10 minutes to re-crisp the mash. You can add a little extra harissa on top of the mash before baking if you want more heat on the surface.
